title: The Social and State Structure of the USSR
description: The Social and State Structure of the USSR is a non-fiction by Alexander Karpinsky. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.
descriptionParagraphs: The Social and State Structure of the USSR is a non-fiction by Alexander Karpinsky. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Singapore. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: The Social and State Structure of the USSR is a non-fiction by Alexander Karpinsky. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.
banningParagraphs: The Social and State Structure of the USSR entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Singapore, where Singapore authorities banned publication or circulation. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
